title: Simon the Coldheart
description: Simon the Coldheart — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Simon the Coldheart" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "To the memory of **my father﻿— this, his favorite**."

memes.label: 'Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents for Simon the Coldheart'

memes.label: "Alan of Montlice"
memes.description: "Son and heir of Fulk of Montlice, Simon's first and closest friend — a gentle, musical knight whose warmth and loyalty balance Simon's coldness through campaigns from Shrewsbury to Normandy, named 'my Poet' by King Henry V himself."

memes.label: "Allegiance"
memes.description: "The bond of loyalty binding a vassal to his lord — the fundamental political relationship of the feudal world, without which neither armies nor kingdoms can function."

memes.label: "Armour"
memes.description: "The protective body-covering worn by knights in battle, which in the novel serves both as practical equipment and as a potent symbol of martial identity."

memes.label: Balderin
memes.description: A French village approximately two and a half leagues from Belrémy, where the Lady Margaret and Jeanne stopped during their escape.

memes.label: Barminster
memes.description: An English estate mentioned in connection with the nobility of the realm.

memes.label: "Barony"
memes.description: "A territorial unit of feudal landholding — the estate held by a baron from the crown, representing the economic and political foundation of noble power in the novel's world."

memes.label: "Battle"
memes.description: "Organised armed conflict between opposing forces, as depicted in the novel's 15th-century military campaigns — the crucible in which Simon's martial identity is tested and proven."

memes.label: "Battle of Shrewsbury"
memes.description: "The Battle of Shrewsbury, fought on 21 July 1403, where King Henry IV defeated the rebel army of Sir Henry 'Hotspur' Percy — the formative military experience of Simon of Beauvallet, who earned his knighthood on the field."

memes.label: Bayeux
memes.description: A town in Normandy where King Henry V held court during Simon's campaign.

memes.label: "Beauvallet"
memes.description: "The estate and barony in Suffolk granted by King Henry IV to Simon of Beauvallet in recognition of his military service -- formerly the rebel-held Fair Pastures, transformed by Simon into a loyal and well-ordered domain."

memes.label: "Belrémy"
memes.description: "A fortified castle-town in Normandy, ruled by the Lady Margaret, besieged and captured by Simon of Beauvallet in 1417."

memes.label: "Belrémy Garrison"
memes.description: "The garrison force defending Belrémy under the Count's command, which opposed Simon's besieging forces."

memes.label: "Campaign in Normandy"
memes.description: "Gloucester's campaign to subdue the Côtentin peninsula, in which Simon serves."

memes.label: "Cedric"
memes.description: "a knight in attendance."

Characters
memes.description: An index of all named characters in *Simon the Coldheart*, dynamically rendered from the wiki's person pages.

memes.label: "Charles of Granmere"
memes.description: "Lord of Granmere, a loyal friend and ally to Simon of Beauvallet who accompanies him on key missions from Shrewsbury onward and serves as a trusted companion throughout the early campaigns."

memes.label: "Cherbourg"
memes.description: "029-054:A heavily fortified town in Normandy that Gloucester plans to besiege."

memes.label: "Chivalry"
memes.description: "The code of conduct governing knights — combining martial prowess, personal honour, and courtesy — which the novel both invokes and critically examines through Simon's cold-hearted character and his insistence on substance over ceremony."

memes.label: "Duel with Raoul the Terrible"
memes.description: "Simon of Beauvallet's single combat with Raoul the Terrible, the mercenary captain who ambushed the Lady Margaret in the woods of Normandy; the duel resulted in Raoul's defeat and the rescue of the captive Countess."

memes.label: Edmund of Fenton
memes.description: The aged and incompetent Marshal of Montlice whom Simon of Beauvallet replaced after taking lordship, representing the decay of the old order before Simon's reforms.

memes.label: Events
memes.description: An index of all significant events in *Simon the Coldheart*, dynamically rendered from the wiki's event pages.

memes.label: Fair Pastures
memes.description: An English estate whose lord attends a banquet of the realm's nobility.

memes.label: "Fall of Belrémy"
memes.description: "The final capture of Belrémy by Simon of Beauvallet's forces, concluding the siege and establishing Lancastrian control."

memes.label: "Fealty"
memes.description: "The feudal bond of loyalty and service binding a vassal to his lord — the foundational obligation of the novel's political world, tested at every level from squire to king."

memes.label: "Ferdinand de Valmé"
memes.description: "Sheriff of Belrémy under the Count of Belrémy, responsible for holding the city keys."

memes.label: Fernand de Turincel
memes.description: Duke of Turincel, a Norman nobleman whom the Lady Margaret sought as an ally against Simon of Beauvallet, but who ultimately submitted to Lancastrian authority.

memes.label: Fortification
memes.description: Defensive military structures — walls, towers, and keeps — central to the siege warfare depicted in the novel, particularly at Belrémy.

memes.label: "Fulk of Montlice"
memes.description: "Earl of Montlice — the battle-hardened giant of a lord who takes the young Simon as his squire, becoming his mentor, protector, and first champion in a bond marked by blows, laughter, and fierce loyalty."

memes.label: Galledemaine
memes.description: A location in France associated with the Comtesse de Galledemaine.

memes.label: "Garrison"
memes.description: "A body of troops stationed in a fortress or town to defend it — the permanent military presence that secures conquered territory in the novel's campaigns."

memes.label: "Geoffrey of Malvallet"
memes.description: "Half-brother to Simon of Beauvallet, son of the Lord of Malvallet, a warm-hearted and spirited young knight who fights alongside Simon at Shrewsbury and in the French campaigns."

memes.label: "Gilbert"
memes.description: "a nobleman in the King's council."

memes.label: "Gilbert of Umfraville"
memes.description: "Sir Gilbert of Umfraville, a knight in the Normandy campaign who serves alongside Henry V's forces and is noted among the notable figures at the muster before Belrémy."

memes.label: "Gloucester"
memes.description: "Duke of Gloucester — a historical figure commanding the Norman campaign."

memes.label: "Gountray"
memes.description: "The domain of Maurice of Gountray, captain serving under Simon at Montlice."

memes.label: "Granmere"
memes.description: "Granmere Hall, the estate of Lord Charles of Granmere, Fulk of Montlice's cousin, who hosted Simon's party during their journey."

memes.label: "Henry IV Bolingbroke"
memes.description: "King of England (1399–1413), the usurper who deposed Richard II and established the Lancastrian dynasty; father of Henry V and the sovereign to whom Simon of Beauvallet swears fealty after the Battle of Shrewsbury."

memes.label: "Honour"
memes.description: "The code of personal integrity, reputation, and martial distinction that governs the behaviour of knights and nobles in the novel's 15th-century world."

memes.label: "Hotspur"
memes.description: "Sir Henry Percy (1364–1403), known as Hotspur — a historical figure referenced in the novel as an exemplar of fiery, impulsive knighthood, standing in contrast to Simon's cold discipline."

memes.label: "House of Beauvallet"
memes.description: "The noble house and barony created by King Henry V for Simon of Beauvallet — a lordship earned entirely through merit and conquest, carved from a confiscated rebel estate known as Fair Pastures, symbolizing the rise of a landless bastard to the ranks of the feudal nobility."

memes.label: "House of Belrémy"
memes.description: "The noble house ruling Belrémy castle."

memes.label: "House of Granmere"
memes.description: "The house of Lord Charles of Granmere, cousin to Fulk of Montlice, based at Granmere Hall."

memes.label: "House of Lancaster"
memes.description: "The ruling royal house of England during the novel's events."

memes.label: "House of Malvallet"
memes.description: "The noble house of Malvallet, based at Malvallet estate, whose legitimate heir is Geoffrey of Malvallet."

memes.label: "House of Montlice"
memes.description: "The noble house of Fulk of Montlice — the foster-house that takes Simon in as a landless boy, trains him as a squire, and launches him on his path to knighthood and lordship. One of the great martial houses of the English North, renowned for its fierce warriors and its lion-crested lord."

memes.label: "House of Santoy"
memes.description: "The minor house from which Walter of Santoy originates, providing soldiers to Simon's campaigns."

memes.label: "House of Valme"
memes.description: "The noble house of Valme in Belrémy, whose member Ferdinand de Valmé served as Sheriff of Belrémy."

memes.label: "Jeanne de Faucourt"
memes.description: "Jeanne de Faucourt — the Lady Margaret's lady-in-waiting and lifelong confidante, whose courtship by Geoffrey of Malvallet bridges the English-French divide and whose gentle loyalty provides a moral compass for her proud and warlike mistress."

memes.label: Joulinceaulx
memes.description: A location in France where a festival was held, mentioned during the Lady Margaret's captivity.

memes.label: "Justice"
memes.description: "The administration of law and rightful judgment — in the novel, a function of lordship that Simon exercises with characteristic cold impartiality."

memes.label: "King Henry V"
memes.description: "King Henry V of England — the historical monarch who appears as a character in the novel, recognising Simon's quality, knighting him, directing the Norman campaigns, and bestowing upon him the memorable epithet 'Simon the Coldheart.'"

memes.label: The King's Council
memes.description: The royal advisory body to King Henry V, comprising lords and gentlemen of the realm who deliberated on matters of war, justice, and state at the King's court.

memes.label: "King's Council at Westminster"
memes.description: "The royal council where King Henry IV summoned Fulk of Montlice and where Simon's knighthood was arranged."

memes.label: "Knighthood"
memes.description: "The rank, status, and identity conferred by the ceremony of dubbing — the formal recognition of martial qualification that Simon earnestly seeks and achieves through demonstrated merit, transforming him from a landless squire into Sir Simon of Beauvallet."

memes.label: "Lady Margaret's Escape"
memes.description: "The Lady Margaret's flight from Belrémy after its fall to Simon."

memes.label: "Lenoir"
memes.description: "a soldier who appears on the battlements with Simon."

memes.label: London
memes.description: The capital of England and seat of the Lancastrian court under King Henry V, where Simon of Beauvallet delivered his treasonous prisoner, received the King's thanks, and was granted the barony of Beauvallet — the political centre to which provincial justice flowed and from which royal favour was bestowed.

memes.label: "Malcolm"
memes.description: "Malcolm Clayton, Simon of Beauvallet's squire and trusted attendant."

memes.label: "Malvallet"
memes.description: "The ancestral seat of Geoffrey of Malvallet, Simon's half-brother, located in England."

memes.label: "Maurice"
memes.description: "a knight in Simon's company."

memes.label: "Maurice of Gountray"
memes.description: "Marshal to Simon of Beauvallet and his steward at Beauvallet, a man of fiery temper who proves his loyalty and capability after Simon subdues his rebellious insolence."

memes.label: Mercy
memes.description: The medieval Christian and chivalric virtue of mercy, as exercised by Simon of Beauvallet in granting quarter at Belrémy and as contested by the Lady Margaret during the siege.

memes.label: "Montlice"
memes.description: "The baronial castle of Fulk of Montlice, situated near Cambridge, where Simon of Beauvallet arrived as a boy of fifteen and served as page and squire before rising to knighthood and lordship."

memes.label: The Montlice Guard
memes.description: The garrison of soldiers stationed at Montlice Castle, initially fractious and ill-disciplined, later reformed under Simon of Beauvallet's command and subsequently under Walter of Santoy's captaincy.

memes.label: "Normandy"
memes.description: "The French duchy and contested territory where Simon of Beauvallet conducts his military campaigns in the service of King Henry V, culminating in his appointment as Warden of the Lands and Marches."

memes.label: "Oath"
memes.description: "A solemn sworn promise binding the speaker to a course of action or obligation — the foundational mechanism of feudal loyalty and personal honour in the novel's world."

memes.label: Organizations
memes.description: An index of all organizations in *Simon the Coldheart*, from noble houses to military commands.

memes.label: Places
memes.description: An index of all locations in *Simon the Coldheart*, dynamically rendered from the wiki's place pages.

memes.label: Plot
memes.description: Political conspiracy in the early Lancastrian period, as revealed in the novel through the intercepted letter of the Baron of Crowburg and the wider network of Ricardian loyalists.

memes.label: Ranaud
memes.description: A giant Frenchman who befriends and protects the Lady Margaret during her flight from Raoul, later joining Simon of Beauvallet's service as a trusted guard.

memes.label: "Raoul the Terrible"
memes.description: "A brutal and sadistic lord whose lands lie between Montlice and Turincel; he captures the Lady Margaret and becomes the central antagonist of the Normandy campaign."

memes.label: Razincourt
memes.description: A French village on the Lady Margaret's escape route from Belrémy, where she and Jeanne stopped for dinner.

memes.label: Rescue of the Damsel
memes.description: Simon of Beauvallet's rescue of a kidnapped girl in the woods near Montlice, which led to the discovery of a Lancastrian loyalist plot against the usurper Richard.

memes.label: Richard II
memes.description: The deposed King of England (r. 1377–1399), whose reported survival in Scotland fuelled the Crowburg conspiracy and other plots against the Lancastrian dynasty during the events of the novel.
aliases: Richard by the Grace of God

memes.label: Roger
memes.description: Squire to Simon of Beauvallet during his early campaigns, a loyal if long-suffering companion who accompanied Simon through the rescue of the damsel and the Shrewsbury campaign.

memes.label: "Santoy"
memes.description: "The region or domain from which Walter of Santoy originates, providing soldiers for Simon's campaigns."

memes.label: "Shrewsbury"
memes.description: "003-000:The town where Simon accompanies Fulk on campaign."

memes.label: "Siege"
memes.description: "A medieval military tactic of surrounding and isolating a fortified place to force its surrender — the dominant form of offensive warfare in the novel's 15th-century setting, exemplified by Simon's methodical campaign against Belrémy."

memes.label: "Siege of Belrémy"
memes.description: "The prolonged siege and capture of Belrémy in Normandy, October-December 1417 — Simon of Beauvallet's first major independent command, conducted with an army of fifteen hundred men against the defiant Lady Margaret, Countess of Belrémy."

memes.label: "Simon of Beauvallet"
memes.description: "The protagonist — a cold-hearted, determined young man who rises from penniless wanderer to knight and lord through sheer force of will and martial prowess. Known as 'Simon the Coldheart' for his unyielding discipline and emotional restraint."

memes.label: "Simon's Journey to Normandy"
memes.description: "Simon's journey from Bedford to Cambridge and onward to Montlice in Normandy, where he enters the service of Fulk of Montlice."

memes.label: "Simon's Knighting"
memes.description: "The ceremony in which Simon of Beauvallet is dubbed a knight by King Henry IV at Shrewsbury following the Battle of Shrewsbury, marking his elevation from squire to Sir Simon and recognizing his extraordinary battlefield valor."

memes.label: "Squire"
memes.description: "A young attendant in the service of a knight, learning the arts of war and chivalry — the role Simon occupies at Montlice during his formative years."

memes.label: "Steward"
memes.description: "An officer entrusted with managing a lord's estates and affairs — a position combining administrative authority with personal trust that is essential to the functioning of feudal lordship."

memes.label: "Stronghold"
memes.description: "A fortified place — castle, walled town, or defensive position — that serves as the anchor of military control and noble power in the novel's landscape."

memes.label: Surrender of Belrémy
memes.description: "The formal surrender of the town and castle of Belrémy to Simon of Beauvallet, following the successful siege, in which the Lady Margaret was compelled to yield the keys and swear fealty to King Henry V."

memes.label: Talmayne
memes.description: A minor character mentioned in the novel.

memes.label: "The Baron of Crowburg"
memes.description: "A loyal adherent of the Lancastrian cause, mentioned as a faithful supporter during the Wars."

memes.label: "The Chevalier de Fleurival"
memes.description: "Victor de Fleurival, the Chevalier of Belremy — the Lady Margaret's foppish, treacherous cousin who swears submission to Simon with a rose in his hand and hatred in his eyes, schemes behind his back, hires an assassin, and is banished in disgrace by his own family."

memes.label: "The King's Army"
memes.description: "The English army under King Henry V conducting the Norman campaign of 1417, divided into three divisions and comprising forces under the Duke of Clarence, Simon of Beauvallet, Gloucester, and other lords — the military instrument through which Henry V conquered Normandy."

memes.label: "The Lady Margaret"
memes.description: "The Lady Margaret of Belrémy — Countess, warrior, and the only soul who matches Simon of Beauvallet's cold intensity. Her defiance, escape, capture by Raoul the Terrible, and ultimate choice to yield her heart make her the novel's most complex figure."

memes.label: "The Truce at Montlice"
memes.description: "The diplomatic truce arranged at Montlice between Lancastrian and French forces, ending open hostilities."

memes.label: "Thomas, Duke of Clarence"
memes.description: "Thomas of Lancaster, Duke of Clarence, second son of Henry IV and brother to Henry V, who serves as a commander in the Normandy campaign."

memes.label: Tourdelonne
memes.description: A French location mentioned during the Lady Margaret's flight.

memes.label: Treason
memes.description: The crime of betraying one's lord or sovereign — a capital offense that drives key political tensions in the novel, most notably Fernand de Turincel's betrayal of the Lady Margaret.

memes.label: "Truce"
memes.description: "A temporary suspension of hostilities between warring parties — in the novel, a tactical and diplomatic tool as important as battle itself."

memes.label: Turincel
memes.description: Castle and ducal seat of Fernand de Turincel in Normandy, approximately ten leagues from Belrémy; sought as refuge by the Lady Margaret during her escape, and later visited by Simon of Beauvallet and Geoffrey of Malvallet as royal envoys during the Belrémy campaign.

memes.label: Vazincourt
memes.description: A destination in France sought by the Lady Margaret, home of the Sieur de Larousie.

memes.label: Walter of Santoy
memes.description: Captain of the guard at Montlice under Simon of Beauvallet, who drilled the garrison, led the rearguard at Belrémy, and commanded Simon's personal escort in Normandy.

memes.label: Warfare
memes.description: Military conflict as depicted in the novel — a pragmatic world of sieges, campaigns, and strategic calculation that forms the backdrop to Simon's career.
